claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit that detects and blocks project file mutations from full-tool Claude agents

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() creates SHA-256 digests of all project files; diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after to detect changes
evidence.why: Core boundary audit implementation detects any file mutations during agent execution

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshots taken before/after agent execution; changes detected and reported in result.boundary.changes
evidence.why: Runtime boundary enforcement triggers when agents mutate project files

evidence.summary: Boundary violations generate BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers that prevent PRD readiness
evidence.why: Boundary violations block PRD readiness even when agent results pass
Dispatch plan specifies forbidden project mutations at protocol level

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt() includes 'Boundary: do not modify target project files' in agent instructions
evidence.why: Agent prompts explicitly forbid project mutations in hard rules

evidence.summary: evidenceResultBlockingIssues() generates blockers from missing/conflicting evidence agent results
evidence.why: PRD readiness inspection enforces evidence quality and completeness

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() blocks external-only evidence for existing-project facts
evidence.why: Ensures brownfield claims require project-scoped evidence from code/tests/docs

evidence.summary: Test confirms boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass
evidence.why: Test evidence that boundary audit overrides passing agent results on violations

evidence.summary: Git-backed boundary test confirms untracked project mutations are detected and blocked
evidence.why: Boundary audit works with both tracked and untracked files

evidence.summary: spawnProviderPrompt() handles claude/codex/custom provider execution with timeout and error handling
evidence.why: Provider adapter supports full-tool agent execution while boundary audit prevents mutations

risks: If boundary audit is bypassed or disabled, evidence agents could corrupt target projects with mutations
Codex provider failures in dispatch logs suggest provider compatibility issues that could affect boundary detection
Evidence agent execution requires explicit allowAgentDispatch=true authorization, but misconfiguration could skip this check
missing: Verification that boundary audit runs in all provider execution modes (claude, codex, custom)
Evidence that boundary detection works when agents spawn subprocesses that modify files
Documentation on boundary audit performance characteristics for large repositories

result.notes: Boundary audit implementation is verified in code and tests. The system correctly detects project mutations, generates blockers, and prevents PRD readiness. Acceptance criteria met: explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on target-project mutation.
